name: Standard Meeting Notes
description: A general-purpose template for any meeting. Produces concise, scannable notes with clear decisions and action items.

sections.title: Summary
sections.instruction: Write 2-3 sentences capturing the meeting's purpose and most important outcome. Lead with what was decided or accomplished, not what was discussed. If the meeting had no clear outcome, state the primary topics covered. Do not begin with 'The meeting was held to discuss...'

sections.title: Key Topics
sections.instruction: For each of the 3-5 major topics, write 2-4 sentences of substantive prose. Each topic's coverage must: (a) name at least one specific person, tool, number, or quoted phrase from the transcript; (b) summarize the *substance* of what was argued (the reasoning, not just the subject); (c) note the resolution if any. Do not use meta-descriptions like "X was discussed" — state what was actually said. If a topic has no substantive content in the transcript beyond its name, omit it. Attribute key statements to speakers when labels are available. Use clear topic sentences. Do not use bullet points — write flowing paragraphs under each topic heading.

sections.title: Decisions
sections.instruction: List only finalized decisions where the group reached agreement. Format each as: the decision, followed by brief rationale if stated. Do not include suggestions, proposals still under debate, or hypotheticals. If no decisions were made, omit this section entirely.

sections.title: Action Items
sections.instruction: List concrete commitments where someone explicitly agreed to do something as bullet points. Format each as: '- **Owner** to task description (deadline if mentioned)'. Only include items with a clear owner. If ownership is ambiguous, note it. Do not include wishes, suggestions, or hypotheticals. Do not use a table.

sections.title: Open Questions
sections.instruction: List unresolved questions or topics that need follow-up. Only include items that were explicitly left open or deferred. If everything was resolved, omit this section entirely.
